title: Salvage plan Type LP3 Complementation Procedure
display_title: Salvage plan Type LP3 Complementation Procedure
wikitext:
[]: Salvage plan. Type LP3 Complementation Procedure?             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jun 25, 2010 4:02 am This is something I just noticed while scanning the script for ep20: TEXT: Operation Plan Type EP2 Salvage Work Procedural Outline (and Type LP3 Complementation Procedure)

[]: Re: Salvage plan. Type LP3 Complementation Procedure?             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Jun 25, 2010 7:47 am Monk Ed wrote: That there is a second use of the word "complement" within the show? What could it be referring to in this case?

[]:             Quote Post by sephirotic » Fri Jun 25, 2010 8:33 am That is an interesting find, but we really need to know the original word in Japanese to make conclusions and better analysis

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Jun 25, 2010 9:29 am Couldn't it be that it's a "complementation" to the procedure tried to rescue Yui made by Ritsuko? It's referring to the plan to save Shinji from the inside of EVA-01, right?

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri Jun 25, 2010 9:32 am Hmm, my non existent Japanese skills allowed me for no better than to put "hokan" (補完) into a translator. Amongst the translations it spewed out was "finalizing". So maybe "hokan" can be used here, as in "finalizing" the pilots form again. Or maybe complementing in the sense of bringing his material form and his soul back together, thus making them complement each other?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jun 25, 2010 9:06 pm Just to hop on the very beginning of the fanwank bandwagon, it strikes me that because salvaging IS the exact opposite of complementation that they would definitely be related. Something to do with the idea that if they learn how to separate a soul from another and pull it out then they could figure out how to combine them together; basically, like reverse engineering. What happens to Shinji in ep. 20 (and Yui before that) definitely have complementation-like elements to them.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jun 26, 2010 12:07 am I'm thinking it's the 'complementation procedure' because this is what you do to fix things when a pilot prematurely complements.
